title: ferray-ufunc: no vectorized math library for transcendentals (sleef/svml equivalent)
description:
Goal: 

Acceptance criteria:
- 

NumPy uses Intel SVML (Short Vector Math Library) for vectorized sin, cos, exp, log on x86 when available — this gives 4-8x speedup over scalar libm. The SVML source is vendored in numpy/_core/src/umath/svml/. ferray-ufunc's transcendentals all call scalar functions (core_math::sin, etc.) in a loop. Even with pulp dispatch, the loop body is scalar. Options: (1) use sleef-rs (Rust bindings to SLEEF — portable vectorized math for x86/ARM), (2) write Remez polynomial approximations in pulp SIMD intrinsics (like fast_exp.rs does for exp), (3) link against libmvec (glibc's vectorized math). This is the single biggest performance gap between ferray-ufunc and NumPy for compute-bound workloads.
